data.text: India revamps mutual fund rulebook, opens room for higher gold and silver exposure
data.snippet: India's markets regulator on Thursday overhauled rules for mutual funds, introducing newer categories of schemes, curbing portfolio overlaps and allowing more exposure to gold and silver.

image.text: The logo of Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) is seen on its headquarters in Mumbai

relatedHeadlines.text: India markets regulator changes gold, silver valuation for mutual funds
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's markets regulator on Thursday directed mutual funds to use domestic stock exchange spot prices to value their physical gold and silver holdings from April 1, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: India's new lending curbs seen squeezing trading firms
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Indian central bank's curbs on bank funding for proprietary trading could spur trading firms to shift business offshore and may force smaller players to shut down, executives and analysts said.

relatedHeadlines.text: Indian brokers seek 6-month freeze on RBI curbs on bank lending, document shows
relatedHeadlines.snippet: An Indian brokers' body has sought a six-month pause on rules that will tighten bank lending to stock brokers and other market intermediaries from April 1, according to a document reviewed by Reuters on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: EXCLUSIVE-India regulator stalls new exchanges' entry into options market, sources say
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's market regulator has stopped the country's two newest exchanges from offering trading in equity derivatives, asking them to build their share-trading businesses first, two regulatory sources with direct knowledge of the matter said.

data.text: US commerce secretary, India minister discuss trade days after Trump's tariffs are struck down
data.snippet: India's Trade Minister Piyush Goyal discussed trade and economic partnership with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick over lunch in New Delhi on Thursday, Goyal said in a post on X, days after President Donald Trump's sweeping global tariffs were invalidated.

image.text: Indian flag, 3D-printed miniature model depicting U.S. President Donald Trump and the word "Tariffs", are seen in this illustration taken August 27, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: US sets preliminary duties on solar imports from India, Indonesia and Laos
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The U.S. Commerce Department on Tuesday announced countervailing duties on solar cells and panels imported by companies in India, Indonesia and Laos in an effort to counteract subsidies supporting the Asian industries.

relatedHeadlines.text: US negotiating with India on Venezuelan oil sale, US envoy says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The United States is in "active negotiation" over the sale of Venezuelan oil to India, to help India diversify its sources of crude oil, U.S. Envoy Sergio Gor said on Friday.

relatedHeadlines.text: EU-India trade deal is what world needs, not tariffs, Finland PM Orpo says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The EU-India trade deal will create opportunities and is what the world needs instead of tariffs, Finland's Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said, after meeting with his Indian counterpart Narendra Modi in New Delhi on Wednesday.

data.text: India, Israel to boost defence cooperation, Modi says
data.snippet: India and Israel will pursue joint development, production and transfer of technology in defence, as well as work on a free trade agreement, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on Thursday.

image.text: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi (L) and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu (R) sign the guest book at the Indian Army Cemetery of World War I to honour fallen Indian soldiers, in the Israeli coastal city of Haifa July 6, 2017.

relatedHeadlines.text: India's Modi visits Israel as US-Iran tensions mount
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in Israel on Wednesday for a two-day visit that both countries have cast as a chance to deepen relations, as regional concerns mount over the risk of military conflict between the United States and Iran.

data.text: Pakistan, Afghan forces clash after days of hostilities
data.snippet: Pakistani and Afghan border forces clashed on Thursday night after the Taliban launched what it called retaliatory strikes on Pakistani installations, sharply escalating tensions after days of cross-border hostilities.

relatedHeadlines.text: Pakistan wary of militant attacks after Afghanistan air strikes
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Pakistan has boosted security and arrested dozens of suspects as it fears rising wave of militant attacks following its air strikes in Afghanistan, Pakistan's Junior Interior Minister said on Wednesday. "Our forces are on high-alert to combat any attacks," the minister, Talal Chaudhry, told Reuters. "You know the militants always react whenever we go after their hideouts in Afghanistan." Pakistan carried out air strikes on targets in Afghanistan over the weekend on what it said were militant targets responsible for a spate of recent suicide bombings on Pakistani soil.

relatedHeadlines.text: What's behind the latest tension between Afghanistan and Pakistan?
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Tensions have heated up again between Islamabad and Kabul this week after Pakistan launched airstrikes on militant targets in Afghanistan.

data.text: INDIA STOCKS-Indian benchmarks end flat; HDFC Bank counters PSU banks, pharma gains
data.snippet: Indian stock benchmarks ended little changed in a choppy session Thursday on subdued risk appetite, missing out on broad gains in global markets from Japan to Europe, where the benchmark index hit a record high.

image.text: A bird flies past the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) building in Mumbai, India, January 31, 2020.

relatedHeadlines.text: INDIA RUPEE-Rupee ends a tad higher, interbank paying interest boosts forward premiums
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Indian rupee closed marginally stronger on Thursday, comforted by strength in regional currencies while elevated demand to buy dollars at the daily reference rate kept gains in check.

relatedHeadlines.text: INDIA BONDS-Indian bonds fall ahead of debt auction, growth data
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Indian government bonds ended lower on Thursday after a choppy session, as traders looked ahead to the penultimate debt auction of the current financial year and the first GDP growth data under a new series.

data.text: 'Will it give me a job?': Nepal's election promises don't stop youth exodus
data.snippet: At a training centre in Nepal's capital Kathmandu, Rahul Pariyar carefully hooks his harness to a rope, learning the basic skills he will need for painting walls, cleaning high rises and other construction work in the United Arab Emirates.

image.text: People planning to leave the country and apply for foreign employment in search of better opportunities queue as they prepare for an interview at Motherland Overseas, a recruitment agency, as Nepal's biggest political parties vow to fix the outflow of workers ahead of the elections of the House of Representatives scheduled for March 5, triggered by historic youth-led protests fuelled by the lack of jobs and endemic corruption that forced an elected prime minister to resign, in Kathmandu, Nepal, February 22, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: FACTBOX-Who is contesting Nepal's polls and what is at stake?
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Nepal will hold a national election next month, its first since deadly youth-led anti-graft protests toppled the government of then-Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli in September.

relatedHeadlines.text: NEWSMAKER-Nepal's rapper-mayor in pole position to become prime minister
relatedHeadlines.snippet: After Nepal's historic youth-led uprising last September killed 77 people and forced then-Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli to resign, a 35-year-old rapper-turned-politician posted a typically terse message to millions of followers on social media.

relatedHeadlines.text: Monarchists rally in support of ex-king as Nepal limps toward election
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Thousands of supporters of Nepal’s former king rallied in Kathmandu on Friday to greet its last monarch Gyanendra on his return from holiday, ahead of the first election since anti-graft unrest by Gen Z young people.

data.text: India central bank's additional liquidity support to wind down after March, bankers say
data.snippet: The Indian central bank's additional liquidity support to lenders, which has pushed overnight rates towards the floor of the policy rate corridor in a bid to ease money market stress and improve transmission, is unlikely to extend beyond March, according to bankers.

image.text: A man speaks on his phone as he walks past the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo inside its headquarters in Mumbai, India, February 7, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: India cenbank not looking to revisit lending rules for proprietary traders, brokers, governor says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's central bank is not planning to revisit recently announced rules for bank financing of proprietary traders and brokers, its governor said on Monday.

relatedHeadlines.text: India central bank proposes changes to foreign exchange dealing rules
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's central bank has proposed changes to foreign exchange rules that seek to offer authorized market participants greater flexibility to trade across electronic platforms while retaining key risk management limits.

relatedHeadlines.text: India cenbank eases foreign borrowing rules in boost for corporates
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's central bank has finalised easier rules for foreign borrowings, first proposed last year, allowing corporations greater flexibility in raising debt overseas.

data.text: Bombardier has chance to boost business jet sales in India, says CEO
data.snippet: India's efforts to improve its airport infrastructure will give Bombardier a chance to boost sales of business jets there, chief executive officer Eric Martel said on Thursday.

data.text: Apple in talks with banks to start payment service in India, Bloomberg News reports
data.snippet: Apple is in talks with several Indian banks and global card networks as it prepares to launch its Apple Pay service in India, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

image.text: A smartphone with the Apple Pay logo is placed on a laptop in this illustration taken on July 14, 2021.

data.text: India's federal agency opens second criminal case against Anil Ambani, Reliance Communications
data.snippet: India's federal investigating agency said on Thursday it has opened a second criminal case against industrialist Anil Ambani and his company Reliance Communications following a complaint from Bank of Baroda over alleged diversion of loans exceeding 22.20 billion rupees ($244.21 million).

image.text: Anil Ambani, chairman of the Reliance Anil Dhirubhai Ambani Group, attends the company's annual general meeting in Mumbai, India, September 30, 2019.

data.text: Businessman Gupta refused permission to appeal in Trafigura nickel fraud lawsuit
data.snippet: Indian businessman Prateek Gupta was refused permission by London's High Court on Thursday to appeal against a ruling that he defrauded commodities trader Trafigura over fake nickel cargoes.

image.text: Chairman of UD Group Prateek Gupta poses in his Singapore office December 11, 2015.

data.text: Bangladesh court orders move to seek Interpol red notice for UK lawmaker Siddiq
data.snippet: A court in Bangladesh ordered authorities to seek an Interpol red notice against British lawmaker and former minister Tulip Siddiq on Thursday over alleged corruption linked to a private real estate project in the capital.

image.text: MP Tulip Siddiq attends a news conference with Richard Ratcliffe, the husband of jailed British-Iranian aid worker Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe, in London, Britain October 11, 2019.

data.text: India braces for unusually hot March; wheat, rapeseed crops at risk, sources say
data.snippet: India is likely to record one of its warmest Marches on record, with above-average temperatures forecast in key wheat and rapeseed-growing states, potentially cutting yields, two weather bureau sources said on Thursday.

image.text: A man struggles to cross a road after his slippers got stuck in melted tar on a hot weather day in Ahmedabad, India, May 20, 2022.

data.text: Cricket-India thump Zimbabwe to revive T20 World Cup title defence
data.snippet: A rampant India piled up a record total and handed Zimbabwe a 72-run shellacking in a Super Eight contest on Thursday to revive their Twenty20 World Cup title defence.

relatedHeadlines.text: EXCLUSIVE-Blitzer joins bidding war for Indian Premier League cricket teams, sources say
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Billionaire David Blitzer is eyeing a majority stake in an Indian Premier League cricket team and is holding talks with two franchises, two sources said, stoking a bidding war against Manchester United co-chairman Avram Glazer and Indian tycoons.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
